scenarios.label: Heart Attack
scenarios.physiological_why: Medical anomaly thresholds. Overrides all other classifications.

scenarios.label: Anger
scenarios.physiological_why: High energy, high stress, strong pounding chest.

scenarios.label: Anxiety
scenarios.physiological_why: Fast heart rate, high stress, but shallow breathing/trembling.

scenarios.label: Fear
scenarios.physiological_why: Fight-or-flight triggered. Fast HR, but heart compensates well (HRV stays normal).

scenarios.label: Joy
scenarios.physiological_why: Resting heart rate, but highly relaxed/positive variance.

scenarios.label: Envy
scenarios.physiological_why: The baseline. Awake, alert, but neutral vitals.

scenarios.label: Disgust
scenarios.physiological_why: Unpleasant stimulus causing mild stress, but not enough to spike HR. Strong physical rejection reaction.

scenarios.label: Embarrassment
scenarios.physiological_why: Mild stress, body physically 'withdraws' (shallow breathing/low amplitude).

scenarios.label: Ennui
scenarios.physiological_why: Extremely relaxed, almost asleep. Low energy.

scenarios.label: Sadness
scenarios.physiological_why: Low energy, but accompanied by withdrawal/stress (low variability).
